text:
Once upon a time, there was a frog named Freddy who lived in a marshy area near a quaint little village. Freddy was a quirky frog with big, expressive eyes that seemed to hold a lifetime of unspoken stories.

Every morning, Freddy would gather on the banks of his home, where bubbles from a nearby spring would dance around him. He'd splash them lightly with his tail, creating a symphony that echoed the dawn songs of birds. One particular spring morning, the sky was draped in such a soft palette of colors that it seemed like a painter had just finished up a masterpiece.

Freddy had a unique talent. When he sang, his croak sounded like a lullaby, a tune that would soothe even the most mischievous caterpillars. One of his favorite songs was about dreams, so one afternoon, he decided to croak over a map he found amongst the reeds. The map was old, speckled with history.

While he sang,
